violations.explanation: Inline page.locator() with POM reference used in test body. The page_additions file defines plan_pause_info as a property that should be accessed directly as print_history_page.plan_pause_info, not wrapped in page.locator(). This violates Prohibition 6 which states ZERO page.locator() in test function body.

Inline page.locator() with POM reference used in test body. Should access the property directly as print_history_page.plan_pause_info. This is the same violation as V001 but for the positive visibility check in Step 5.

Incorrect access pattern using .elements. namespace when the locator is defined as a direct property. According to the mapping report and existing PrintHistoryPage structure, print_history_card is accessed as a direct property, not via .elements. The framework uses two patterns: direct properties (self.X = page.locator()) accessed as page_obj.X, and Elements class string selectors accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.X). This code incorrectly mixes both patterns.
Same issue as V003 - using .elements. when should use direct property access. The page_additions file defines these as direct properties in __init__, not as Elements class selectors.

# Precondition 4: Pause the plan
            framework_logger.info("Precondition: Pausing subscription plan")
            GeminiRAHelper.access(page)
            GeminiRAHelper.access_tenant_page(page, tenant_email)
            # Access edit menu and select pause plan event
            page.locator("a:has-text('Edit')").click()
            page.locator("select[name='subscription[event_name]']").select_option("event_pause_plan")
            page.locator("input[type='submit'][value='Save']").click()
            page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle", timeout=30000)
            framework_logger.info("Precondition: Plan paused successfully")

violations.current_code: # Precondition 4: Pause the plan section with inline admin interactions
violations.explanation: Admin/backend interactions for pausing plan should be encapsulated in a helper method, not implemented inline with raw page.locator() chains. This violates the framework pattern of using helpers for all admin operations.

fix_details.code_to_add:
@staticmethod
    def pause_subscription_plan(page):
        """Pauses the subscription plan via Rails Admin."""
        page.locator("a:has-text('Edit')").click()
        page.locator("select[name='subscription[event_name]']").select_option("event_pause_plan")
        page.locator("input[type='submit'][value='Save']").click()
        page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle", timeout=30000)
        framework_logger.info("Subscription plan paused successfully")

violations.current_code:
class Elements:
    # Billing Cycle Period Card locators
    plan_pause_info = "[data-testid='plan-pause-info']"
    billing_cycle_period_card = "[data-testid='billing-cycle-period-card']"
    ...

# ADD TO __init__ METHOD (after self.elements = self.Elements()):

# Billing Cycle Period Card locators as properties
self.plan_pause_info = page.locator(self.elements.plan_pause_info)
self.billing_cycle_period_card = page.locator(self.elements.billing_cycle_period_card)
violations.expected_code: Define locators ONLY in Elements class as string selectors. Do NOT duplicate as properties in __init__. The framework pattern is: Elements class stores strings, accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.X) OR define as direct properties in __init__ without Elements class.
violations.explanation: The page additions file incorrectly defines locators in BOTH the Elements class AND as properties in __init__. This creates confusion and violates the framework pattern. The framework uses two distinct patterns: (1) Elements class with string selectors accessed via page.locator(page_obj.elements.X), or (2) Direct properties in __init__ accessed as page_obj.X. This file mixes both patterns incorrectly. The correct approach for new additions is to define them as direct properties in __init__ ONLY, matching the existing PrintHistoryPage pattern shown in the mapping report.

fix_details.description: Remove the Elements class definition entirely. Keep only the __init__ property definitions. The locators should be defined as direct properties using page.locator() in __init__, not as Elements class attributes.
fix_details.current_structure: class Elements with string selectors + __init__ properties

violations.expected_code: Remove the module-level 'from playwright.sync_api import expect' import. The merge instructions explicitly state to remove this import from inside each method, and it should not be at module level in the additions file either.
violations.explanation: The page additions file has 'from playwright.sync_api import expect' at module level. While the merge instructions say to add it to the target file if not present, the instructions also say to 'Remove from playwright.sync_api import expect from inside each method'. The target file (pages/print_history_page.py) should already have this import. Having it in the additions file is redundant and violates the merge instructions.

fix_priority_order.fix_approach: Replace all occurrences of 'print_history_page.elements.X' with 'print_history_page.X' throughout the test script. The page additions file defines these as direct properties in __init__, not as Elements class selectors. The framework pattern for direct properties is to access them as page_obj.property_name, not page_obj.elements.property_name.

Replace page.locator(print_history_page.elements.X) with direct property access print_history_page.X. Remove the page.locator() wrapper when accessing POM properties.
Remove all TODO/Note comments after implementing the required functionality. These comments indicate incomplete implementation that should be resolved.
Complete the pause plan precondition implementation by adding the actual Rails Admin interaction code or calling the appropriate helper method.
Restructure page additions to use only direct property definitions in __init__, removing the Elements class definition. This matches the existing PrintHistoryPage pattern.
Remove the module-level 'from playwright.sync_api import expect' import from the page additions file as it will be present in the target file.
